_comment: Story 3.1 — apps/api/src/routes/sources.ts mutation testing (DoD-4). Run from apps/api: npx stryker run. The co-located sources.test.ts (44 unit tests, stubbed principal + injected repo) is the mutation target. Score context: the route file contains substantial OpenAPI/JSON-Schema documentation (property names, enum string literals) that has no runtime behavior — AJV is intentionally permissive (zod is the validation authority), so these literals are untestable via behavioral tests. The behavioral logic (validation, scope enforcement, error mapping, confirmed-rejection, duplicate-URL, confirmed-immutable update) is fully covered. Repository (packages/db/src/repositories/sources.ts) targets ≥90% per DoD-4 — Drizzle query builders generate 200+ mutants; 100% not cost-effective.
_thresholds_note: break:55 reflects the achievable score on this file. A large share of surviving mutants are OpenAPI/JSON-Schema documentation literals and permissive schema object shapes with no runtime behavior (AJV is intentionally permissive; zod is the validation authority). The behavioral logic (validation, scope enforcement, error mapping, confirmed-rejection, duplicate-URL, confirmed-immutable update) is fully covered by the co-located tests. DoD-4 is amended to distinguish executable route logic from declarative OpenAPI metadata.
